mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in retail sales signals strengthening consumer demand that drives corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales registered 535982 million dollars in July 2020
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and 20-day S&P 500 forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8%, fiscal and monetary policy backstops dominate earnings deterioration, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as liquidity effects compress the equity risk premium.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in industrial production and a federal funds rate below 0.25% jointly signal manufacturing recovery amplified by negligible borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week that exceed either variable in isolation.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 100.1665 in July 2020 with fed funds at 0.09%
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO changes and FEDFUNDS levels does not add incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 2.0) for 5-day S&P 500 returns in a bivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery, quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because earnings expectations rebase from depressed base levels and discount rates remain anchored.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2020 real GDP at 17205.8 billion chained 2017 dollars
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of post-shock quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in the consumer price index signals demand-pull pricing power that expands nominal corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes and 5-day S&P 500 forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.1 million units signal durable construction demand and housing-related consumption spillovers that lift cyclical earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts at 1186 thousand units in June 2020
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST exceeds 1100 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls combined with a federal funds rate below 0.25% jointly signal a labor market recovery supported by accommodative policy, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month that neither variable generates independently.
evidence_refs: fact:Nonfarm payrolls at 139582 thousand in July 2020 with fed funds at 0.09%
failure_condition: The joint condition of positive PAYEMS changes and FEDFUNDS below 0.25% does not produce significantly higher average 20-day returns than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 2% amid near-zero policy rates forces eventual monetary tightening expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as forward earnings multiples compress.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2020 CPI at 258.723 with fed funds at 0.09%
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of periods when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.
